preamble: The shift from the rhotic consonant [r] to a retroflex stop like [ʈ] (or [ɖ]) typically occurs through sandhi assimilation. In languages with retroflex phonology, an [r] preceding a dental or alveolar stop causes the articulation to migrate backward, merging the two sounds into a single retroflex stop.

examples.note: Recursive sandhi: the [r] merges with the following [t] to create a voiceless retroflex stop.
Historical retroflexion of dental stops triggered by a preceding rhotic.
